name: PocketCursor Bridge
tagline: Bridge Telegram group chat to your Cursor workflow
description: A self-hosted Telegram bridge service with multi-user group support and a web admin UI for authorization and verbosity controls. The Python CDP bridge (pocket_cursor.py, start_cursor.py, etc.) lives in this package for local development machines; the Docker image runs the Flask admin and Telegram polling worker.

stack.backend: Python Flask + Telegram Bot API polling worker

env_vars.description: Telegram bot token used for bridge polling and replies.
Your Telegram user id (numeric). Local pocket_cursor.py locks the bridge to this user; set here once and sync to your Mac with scripts/sync-pocket-bridge-env.sh or POCKET_SYNC_FROM_ADMIN=1.
Optional fixed Telegram chat/group id to listen to (blank accepts any authorized chat).
Comma-separated allowed Telegram user IDs for explicit whitelist.
Flask bridge admin log level; default verbose. Local pocket_cursor.py: same modes — POCKETCURSOR_BRIDGE_VERBOSITY overrides when set.
Local CDP bridge: quiet | normal | verbose (default verbose — live thinking stream). Overrides POCKETCURSOR_VERBOSITY when set.
If true, only whitelisted users can use the bridge in group chats.
